subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/ECF, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Ghanaian official (GSS, Bank of Ghana, GoldBod) + Ghanaian independent press (MyJoyOnline, Citi, GhanaWeb).

A stable democracy executing a credible recovery from a 2022 default: the Dec-2024 election produced a peaceful turnover, with sharp disinflation, a gold-led reserve rebuild, and a falling debt ratio.

Standout features: democratic stability and a real recovery, against high debt, commodity dependence, illegal-mining/climate damage, and Sahel-spillover risk. Source-lean: HIGH but low-distortion Anglophone.

economic.notes: A credible post-default recovery - sharp disinflation, a stabilised cedi, a gold-led reserve rebuild - against a high debt load and commodity exposure.

fiscal_state.notes: Defaulted in 2022 but with the IMF program's reviews on track, a primary surplus overperforming, and the debt ratio dropping, though restructuring is not fully closed. (IMF)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible disinflation and a stabilised cedi - a reform-era anchor. (IMF; Bank of Ghana)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The cedi is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Down from a ~50% peak to low single digits on a 15-month downtrend, with durability the watch-item. (GSS; Bloomberg)

banking_stability.notes: Recapitalising after the domestic-debt-restructuring hit, profitable but with some banks below minimums. (IMF; State Dept ICS)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Gold (a record-boosted exporter) plus cocoa (crop-struggling) and oil, with a gold-led reserve rebuild a 2025 strength; commodity-cycle exposed. (GoldBod; USDA)

institutional.notes: Relatively strong regional institutions - functional courts, credible statistics, and a free press - with corruption persisting.

rule_of_law.notes: Relatively strong for the region - functional courts - though corruption persists. (WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: The statistics service is credible and IMF-engaged. (GSS)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Functional but with mixed delivery. (WJP; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Relatively free and vibrant (RSF ~39/180, its strongest in years), with some pressure. (RSF)

political_social.notes: A mature democracy with a peaceful turnover and strong liberties, against hardship discontent and localised unrest.

political_stability.notes: A mature democracy - the Dec-2024 election produced a peaceful turnover (the fifth since 1992) - against economic-hardship discontent. (Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong by regional standards, with some shrinking civic space. (WJP; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful routes with a diaspora-welcoming tilt and a regional-hub role. (observed)

social_cohesion.notes: Broadly cohesive, with a localised conflict, illegal-mining unrest, and Sahel spillover. (ADF)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing - a dividend with a jobs strain. (UN)

geopolitical.notes: A stable Western partner and ECOWAS anchor, against rising Sahel-spillover risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable Western/US partner and ECOWAS anchor - pragmatic. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: No internal war, but rising Sahel-jihadist spillover risk on the northern border. (ADF)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open and unsanctioned, with crisis-era controls easing. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Decent regional infrastructure and relative safety, against flooding and illegal-mining environmental damage.

climate.notes: Coastal erosion (Keta/Volta) and northern drought, with relatively better governance/readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: A near-universal health-insurance scheme but underfunded and uneven. (observed)

infrastructure.notes: Decent regionally - roads, ports, power - though load-shedding recurs. (observed)

crime_safety.notes: Relatively safe, with localised northern risk. (observed)

summary: A mature, stable democracy executing a credible recovery from its 2022 default: the December-2024 election delivered a peaceful turnover (the fifth since 1992), inflation has fallen sharply from a ~50% peak, the cedi has stabilised, and a gold-led reserve rebuild has cut the debt ratio. Institutions are relatively strong for the region - functional courts, credible statistics, and a free, vibrant press. The drags are a still-high debt load, commodity-cycle dependence (gold, cocoa, oil), illegal-mining environmental damage and flooding, recurrent load-shedding, and rising jihadist-spillover risk on the northern border.
